messages.content: hi
Hello! How can I assist you today?

from context: An adverse event (AE) is defined as untoward medical occurrence in a participant administered a pharmaceutical product and does not necessarily have a causal relationship with study treatment. An AE can therefore be any unfavorable or unintended sign (eg, an abnormal laboratory finding), symptom, or disease temporally associated with the use of a medicinal product, whether or not considered related to the medicinal product. AE relationship to study treatment will be assessed as not related or related to study treatment. AE severity is assigned to 1 of the following categories from National Cancer Institute Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE): Grade 1 (mild), Grade 2 (moderate), Grade 3 (severe), Grade 4 (life-threatening), and Grade 5 (fatal). Each CTCAE term is a Lowest Level Term per Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA). Each Lowest Level Term will be coded to a MedDRA Preferred Term (PT).Adverse events of special interest (AESI) will include meningococcal infections in this study.
answer question: What Adverse events of special interest in this study?
The adverse events of special interest in this study include meningococcal infections.

from context: A listing of patient deaths and cause of death will be produced, if applicable.Treatment-emergent adverse events of special interest (TEAESIs) will be summarized by treatment group in tabular form. There is only one AESI: Meningococcal Infections.
answer question:  What Adverse events of special interest in this study?
and remember answer shortly.
Meningococcal infections.

from context: A listing of patient deaths and cause of death will be produced, if applicable.Treatment-emergent adverse events of special interest (TEAESIs) will be summarized in tabular form. The following provides a list of adverse events of special interest (AESI):  Infections:  Meningococcal infections  Aspergillus infections  Other serious infections  Sepsis  Infusion reactions  Angioedema TEAESIs will be summarized by SOC/PT, by PT alone and by relationship.
answer question: What Adverse events of special interest in this study?
and remember answer shortly.

The adverse events of special interest in this study are:
- Meningococcal infections
- Aspergillus infections
- Other serious infections
- Sepsis
- Infusion reactions
- Angioedema
more short answer
The adverse events of special interest in this study include meningococcal infections, Aspergillus infections, other serious infections, sepsis, infusion reactions, and angioedema.
more short
Meningococcal infections, Aspergillus infections, other serious infections, sepsis, infusion reactions, and angioedema.
